name: IRC_LC_EVENT_MSG
description: Table for storing event messages the ESS Job will be processing.

columns.Name: EVENT_CODE
columns.Comments:
Predefined event code following the source of the event

Ex : Code identifying Assessment request updates, Background check result update etc.

columns.Name: CONTEXT_ID
columns.Comments:
References a lifecycle context object for which the event is applicable.

Relation component 1/2

Ex: SubmissionId, Requisition ID, pool member Id

columns.Name: CONTEXT_TYPE
columns.Comments:
Lifecycle context type.

Relation component 2/2

Ex : 'CSW', 'POOL', 'REQUISITION'

columns.Name: TARGET_PROCESSING_DATE
columns.Comments:
Date at which we want event to be processed

(For timer events typically)

columns.Name: ERROR_MESSAGE
columns.Comments: Error message in case message handling is failing.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: EVENT_TYPE
columns.Comments: Defining the type of event. Possible values are EVENT or ASYNC_ACTION.

columns.Name: ACTION_PARAMS
columns.Comments: Column storing the action parameters Map.
